purpose: add_rule_from_json's malformed arms: missing file ('JSON file not found: <path>'); invalid JSON syntax (ValueError embedding json's position message); a non-array document ('JSON file must contain an array of rule objects, got dict'); a string item (the doubled item prefix "Item 0: Failed to parse rule - Item 0: Expected object, got str" — the inner message is re-wrapped at pyreason.py:1064); a threshold object missing 'thresh' ("Item 0: Failed to parse rule - Item 0, threshold 0: Invalid threshold - 'thresh'" — the KeyError's key surfaces verbatim, inside the same outer wrap as every loader-level ValueError); and a non-integer custom_thresholds dict key ("Item 0: Failed to parse rule - Item 0: custom_thresholds dict key 'one' must be an integer clause index" — doubled prefix). All six raise before any add_rule — pinned by the closing fingerprint returning null (no rule global was ever initialized).
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_rule_from_json malformed-* classes; pyreason.py:944-1080 at the pin; exception text screened live 2026-07-07
